review: Spotify is easy to use and nice, as long as you have Premium. Got the free trial, and the overall experience was flawless, though I wish they had an automated flawless AutoMix feature like Apple Music. On the other hand, free Spotify is a nightmare and makes some feel miserable. Ads are fine, sometimes the same ad repeats twice in a row which is a little annoying, but getting ads every less than the 15 minutes advertised is more annoying. Another thing is the fixed shuffle mode. Artists carefully craft album tracklists for everyone to enjoy, but then Spotify ruins that and puts unrelated tracks on top of that. Additionally, Spotify also forces in unrelated songs that you as an user haven’t listened to into a public playlist you may be listening to, once I got 12 tracks (equal to 40mins or more) before Spotify queued up the songs left from the playlist. Skips are useful for those cases, but those are still very limited and capped at 6 per hour. As a user, you have to find workarounds, like making a playlist with an album you may want to listen to instead of listening to it directly to avoid the unrelated songs from queueing up, but even that does not prevent anything if the playlist is short. The UI could improve too, maybe used Liquid Glass or just modernize it and make it look less full. Wish there was a paid tier below Premium that’s not the current student discount.

review:
So my Spotify app updated today on my iPhone, and now the “like” button is basically hidden unless I fully open the app, tap the little bar at the bottom showing what’s currently playing, and then open the full-screen player just to save a song. Seriously, why make something so simple so complicated?

For a music app, quick access to the like/add button should be basic functionality. I shouldn’t have to jump through multiple steps just to save a song I like. This update honestly made the app more frustrating to use, not better. 
& If saving a song now requires a whole side quest, I might as well cancel my subscription.

Amazing innovation, Spotify. Really groundbreaking stuff. NOT.

review:
Spotify has rapidly declined since integrating AI and forcing video content. They have a long history of terrible customer service but the company is increasingly predatory in new ways. They want to be the next big social media platform but the ambition is poorly integrated and insulting to users. 

They are chasing investors by inflating growth numbers via increased pricing, no human staff, and reaching for new subscribers in Brazil and South Africa while North American and Canadian subscribers are bailing for better audio-only platforms.

If you want music and podcasts go to any other service, Spotify is an overpriced mess with reposted YouTube content and an affinity for AI slop.
title: Only getting worse (long-time premium subscriber)

review: It’s absolutely insane to me that other members of a premium family plan outside of the plan manager (PM) can’t be given any kind of permissions to manage a child’s account on the same plan. My plan was set up by someone else in the family, before a kid came into the picture, and you’re telling me the only workaround to give me the permissions I need is to have the current PM nuke the entire plan and set everyone up all over again with me as the PM? My husband can’t even have the ability to change things without me (or current PM) doing it for him through their account? Why? Just let the PM assign roles/permissions to other adults as they see fit, and then the issue resolves itself.
title: Really Should Implement This; Annoying

review: Yes

It feels like everytime I go to play something on Spotify more and more stuff is locked behind a paywall. I’ve used Spotify for years, going on and off of premium multiple times. It’s never felt like such a useless app before now. They used to let you listen to music for a reasonable amount of time and then would play a reasonable amount of ads. That’s not a thing anymore. It plays ads more frequently than the app says it does, it plays either too many ads or too long. It’s just dumb I’m better off using YouTube to listen to music bc at least I can listen to a album there without having a random song I’ve never listened to, never liked, and don’t want to hear play in between EVERY single song on the album. Even having premium doesn’t feel worth it, it cost too much and instead of getting new features they just keep locking more of the things they already had for free behind the paywall. It’s ridiculous and not even close to worth the space it takes up on my phone. I doubt this app will ever be even a slight bit close to what it used to be and that’s a shame.
